top5Opportunities.statement: Increase the ability to qualify a new stainless steel instrument profile supplier to the approved supplier list within 6 months, including ISO 13485 audit and first-article approval
Minimize batch-to-batch variability in inclusion content (non-metallic inclusion rating per ISO 4967) of instrument-grade stainless steel profiles across consecutive deliveries
Minimize the likelihood of sole-source medical-grade profile supplier dependency causing instrument production stoppages exceeding 10 days per year
Minimize variability in cross-section dimensional accuracy between bars within the same delivery batch of instrument-grade stainless profile, measured to +-0.02 mm
Increase the ability to achieve first-pass yield above 97% on forged or machined instrument blanks by eliminating rework caused by profile stock dimensional or metallurgical variability

needs.statement: Increase the ability to qualify a new stainless steel instrument profile supplier to the approved supplier list within 6 months, including ISO 13485 audit and first-article approval
needs.importanceRationale: Multi-month supplier qualification in a regulated environment is the primary barrier to new profile adoption.

needs.statement: Minimize batch-to-batch variability in inclusion content (non-metallic inclusion rating per ISO 4967) of instrument-grade stainless steel profiles across consecutive deliveries
needs.importanceRationale: Inclusion variability directly affects heat treatment response, edge retention, and sterilization durability of finished instruments.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of sole-source medical-grade profile supplier dependency causing instrument production stoppages exceeding 10 days per year
needs.importanceRationale: Die-specific geometry + ISO 13485 qualification creates inherent sole-source risk.

needs.statement: Minimize variability in cross-section dimensional accuracy between bars within the same delivery batch of instrument-grade stainless profile, measured to +-0.02 mm
needs.importanceRationale: Fixed carbide die enforces identical cross-section bar-to-bar; cold-drawing process physics eliminate dimensional scatter.

needs.statement: Increase the ability to achieve first-pass yield above 97% on forged or machined instrument blanks by eliminating rework caused by profile stock dimensional or metallurgical variability
needs.importanceRationale: First-pass yield directly drives instrument production economics and delivery schedule.
